title: Immersive Navigation
description: Adds multiple navigation tools to avoid F3 screen.
body:
![Title](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/7eb35d7ca0efd8477cb6798378ae72d796e93e1f.png)
# Immersive Navigation

Immersive Navigation is a utility and quality-of-life mod for Minecraft that enhances exploration and immersion by providing a variety of tools to help navigate and understand the world, without relying on debug screens (F3). These tools simulate semi-realistic navigation and surveying experiences while maintaining gameplay immersion.
## Features

    Depth Meter: Displays the player's current Y-level (height).
    Cartesian Tool: Shows the player's X and Z coordinates or chunk information.
    Digital Clock: Shows the time in modern language. (12-Hour)
    Metal Detector: Detects nearby ores.
    Moist Sensor: Determines if the player is standing in a slime chunk **(My Favorite)**.
    Hollow Scanner: Identifies open areas (2-block tall) beneath the player.
    Terrain Analyzer: Detects if valuable materials exist under the player, summarizes the richness.
   ** Omni-Gauge**: All-in-one tool with switchable modes for multiple functionalities like time display, coordinate checking, and terrain analysis.

## Crafting
    Battery: Crafted using lapis lazuli and iron ingots.
    Solar Panel: Renewable energy component crafted using glass, iron ingots, and sunlight sensor.

### Not-so-near Plans

    Config for ore detection, compatibility with other mods.

### How to Use

    All tools: Right-click to for functionality.
    Omni-Gauge: Shift + Right-click to change mode.

### Installation

    If not from modrinth client:
    Download and install Fabric and Fabric API.
    Place the mod .jar file into your mods folder located in .minecraft.
